title: pedicularis palustris subsp. borealis
intro: Pedicularis palustris subsp. borealis, also known as the northern pedicularis, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. The plant is valued for its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve anxiety and insomnia, and alleviate menstrual cramps. It can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal effects.
